steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Walsh found that social dimensions made users "feel more mindful of how their listening activities may be perceived," which then changed how they managed those activities. The right answer must show a user self-consciously adjusting listening behavior because of awareness of social observation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'passive streaming': Describes a user passively playing music without any mention of social awareness or behavior adjustment. ✗
  • Choice B — 'selecting songs for what followers might like': Shows the speaker choosing music based on what "friends who are following me might be interested in" rather than personal preference — exactly the perception-driven behavior shift Walsh documented. ✓
  • Choice C — 'algorithmic recommendations': Focuses on the platform's algorithm, not the user's social self-awareness. ✗
  • Choice D — 'social connection': Discusses connecting with others generally but not the conscious modification of listening driven by how others perceive it. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
